source: docs/SHB_Corporate_Sales_MVP_Data_Blueprint_V3_Proposal.docx section 9.10 ('12 cases: Pass, block, missing, stale, conflict') cross-referenced against data/synthetic/v3/legal/eligibility_rules.json and data/synthetic/v3/shared/companies.json built earlier in this same pack.
note: context_overrides only apply for the scope of that one scenario -- they do NOT mutate the base company record in companies.json. status values follow blueprint section 11.2 (passed/failed/pending_information/pending_review).

scenarios.notes: Only required_document rule applies; business_registration verified.
service_agreement not present in the base company record -- required_document rule for SYNTH-PROD-BULK-PAYMENT.
account_or_unit_count 6 >= 2 (warning-severity rule passes cleanly, included to prove a warning rule does not block when satisfied).
account_or_unit_count 1 < 2 triggers the warning-severity rule, but warning severity must not flip overall_status to failed/pending_information -- distinguishes warning from blocking severity.
Hero case's actual blocking pair per blueprint section 3.1/9.12: missing financial_statements and ubo_declaration. operating_years and bad_debt both pass.

has_bad_debt_12m=true is a hard block (on_unknown=pending_review, but a KNOWN true value is a factual failure, not an unknown) -- all documents present and verified, so this isolates the bad-debt rule specifically.
business_registration verified; bad-debt rule is scoped only to SYNTH-PROD-WORKING-CAPITAL, must not leak into unrelated products.

A previously-verified business_registration whose status is overridden to 'expired' for this scenario must be treated the same as missing (required_document operator only accepts 'verified'), not silently reused because a prior verified copy once existed -- staleness case per blueprint section 9.9 ('Freshness: TTL/update/effective date... Mark stale; block time-sensitive decision').

CRM says 500, an uploaded document says 450 -- section 9.9 ('Uniqueness/consistency: CRM-DMS-registry conflicts -> Conflict report/pending review') requires surfacing the conflict rather than silently picking either source; both values still individually satisfy the >=10 threshold so this tests conflict SURFACING independent of whether the rule itself would still pass.

COMP-XYZ has no financial_statements/ubo_declaration documents in the base record (never requested credit before) -- second, independent pending_information case on a different company/product pair than SCN-ELIG-007, to guard against a fix that only special-cases COMP-ABC.
